K.description: Number of trucks/vehicles in the fleet

N.description: Number of pickup and delivery jobs

d.description: Travel time from job i's return terminal to job j's pickup terminal; d[i][i] is the loaded time (rejection penalty) of job i

d0.description: Travel time from the home terminal of truck k to job i's pickup terminal

dH.description: Travel time from job i's return terminal to the home terminal of truck k

v.description: Time at which each truck becomes available

tau_min.description: Earliest permissible arrival time at each job's pickup terminal

tau_max.description: Latest permissible arrival time at each job's pickup terminal

assumptions.description: All travel times between jobs are strictly positive.

code.python: assert all(d[i][j] > 0 for i in range(N) for j in range(N) if i != j)

assumptions.description: Triangle inequality for depot-to-job travel: going directly to job i is no longer than going via job j first.
assumptions.formulation: d^k_{0i} \leq d^k_{0j} + d_{ji} \quad \forall k \in \{0,\dots,K-1\},\, i, j \in \{0,\dots,N-1\}

code.python: assert all(d0[k][i] <= d0[k][j] + d[j][i] for k in range(K) for i in range(N) for j in range(N))

assumptions.formulation: d_{ij} \leq d_{im} + d_{mj} \quad \forall i, j, m \in \{0,\dots,N-1\}

code.python: assert all(d[i][j] <= d[i][m] + d[m][j] for i in range(N) for j in range(N) for m in range(N))

assumptions.description: Earliest permissible arrival times are nonnegative.

code.python: assert all(tau_min[i] >= 0 for i in range(N))

assumptions.description: Latest permissible arrival times are nonnegative.

code.python: assert all(tau_max[i] >= 0 for i in range(N))

EST.description: Earliest start time for job i: maximum of its time-window lower bound and the earliest any truck can arrive

code.python: EST = [max(tau_min[i], min(v[k] + d0[k][i] for k in range(K))) for i in range(N)]

A_minus.description: Set of infeasible sequencing pairs (i,j): job j cannot follow job i without violating j's time window

code.python: A_minus = {(i, j) for i in range(N) for j in range(N) if EST[i] + d[i][i] + d[i][j] > tau_max[j]}

F2.description: Set of mutually feasible pairs: distinct jobs where neither sequencing direction is in A_minus

code.python: F2 = {(i, j) for i in range(N) for j in range(N) if i != j and (i, j) not in A_minus and (j, i) not in A_minus}

M.description: Big-M constant for arrival-time propagation: upper bound on any feasible arrival time

code.python: M = 2 * max(d[i][j] for i in range(N) for j in range(N))

x.description: 1 if arc (u,v) is selected in the routing cycle cover; x[K+i][K+i]=1 means job i is rejected

delta.description: Arrival time at job i's pickup terminal

constraints.description: Each node has exactly one outgoing arc.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(gp.quicksum(x[u, v] for v in range(K + N)) == 1 for u in range(K + N))

constraints.description: Each node has exactly one incoming arc.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(gp.quicksum(x[v, u] for v in range(K + N)) == 1 for u in range(K + N))

constraints.description: Arrival time at job i is at least the earliest possible arrival from any truck.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(delta[i] - gp.quicksum((d0[k][i] + v[k]) * x[k, K + i] for k in range(K)) >= 0 for i in range(N))

constraints.description: Arrival time propagation between consecutive jobs via big-M.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(delta[j] - delta[i] - M * x[K + i, K + j] + (d[i][i] + d[i][j]) * x[K + i, K + i] >= d[i][i] + d[i][j] - M for i in range(N) for j in range(N))

constraints.description: Time-window feasibility for each job's pickup.

code.gurobipy:
model.addConstrs(delta[i] >= tau_min[i] for i in range(N))
model.addConstrs(delta[i] <= tau_max[i] for i in range(N))

constraints.description: EC2: For each mutually feasible pair (i,j), at most one of the two sequencing directions or a rejection of i can hold.

code.gurobipy:
for i, j in F2:
    model.addConstr(x[K + i, K + j] + x[K + j, K + i] + x[K + i, K + i] <= 1)

objective.description: Minimize total travel cost: deadhead from truck homes, inter-job travel, and returns to truck homes.

code.gurobipy:
model.setObjective(
    gp.quicksum(d0[k][i] * x[k, K + i] for k in range(K) for i in range(N))
    + gp.quicksum(d[i][j] * x[K + i, K + j] for i in range(N) for j in range(N))
    + gp.quicksum(dH[k][i] * x[K + i, k] for i in range(N) for k in range(K)),
    GRB.MINIMIZE
)
